page: Microsoft Azure E4as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor running at 3.35 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server includes 66 GB of local storage, is Premium Storage capable, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The E4as_v4 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and average single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance is weak, as evidenced by lower scores in multi-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with uncached large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10% of tested systems.

Given its high memory density relative to its compute resources, the E4as_v4 offers a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high parallel processing power. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, text processing, and basic web serving. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded compilation, complex ray tracing, or intensive LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, delivering a memory-optimized 8.0 GB per core ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor running at 3.35 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 66 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile makes the E4as_v4 a qualitatively cost-effective choice for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel computing. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and text processing applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for memory-bound workloads.
